aiReadyLines.text: 6 English For Today
Let's now see what we say when we hear about something not good
or not happy. Read the following dialogues.
Bobby Hi, Sujan! Why didn’t you come
to school last week?
Sujan I had the flu. I was in bed
most of the time.
Bobby Oh, I'm sorry to hear that.
How do you feel now?
Shakil Hello, Manik! Why are you so quiet today?
Manik Did you see that pen my father gave me?
I've lost it. It was very dear to me!
Shakil Oh, I'm so sorry! How did you lose it?
Manik I don't know! I think I dropped it somewhere.
Shanu Hi, Munira! Have you seen the result?
Munira I've failed in my math test.
Shanu Oh, that's bad luck!
Munira —_ don’t know how to tell my parents.
Shanu T think they will understand.
I know you'll do better next time.
Language Focus
When we hear about any bad news, we say, 'I'm sorry to hear that’
or ‘That's bad luck’, etc. In response, the other person usually doesn't
BI. Practise saying the above conversations in pairs.
B2. Answer the following questions.
What would you say to somebody who:
has won a lottery?
b has lost a football match?
c just congratulated you on your good result?
d__ has been ill?
e has been selected for a music programme on TV?
B3. Work with a partner. Make short conversations on the situations

rawText:
6 English For Today
B. Let's now see what we say when we hear about something not good
or not happy. Read the following dialogues.
i) Bobby Hi, Sujan! Why didn’t you come
to school last week?
Sujan I had the flu. I was in bed
most of the time.
Bobby Oh, I'm sorry to hear that.
How do you feel now?
ii) Shakil Hello, Manik! Why are you so quiet today?
Manik Did you see that pen my father gave me?
I've lost it. It was very dear to me!
Shakil Oh, I'm so sorry! How did you lose it?
Manik I don't know! I think I dropped it somewhere.
iii) Shanu Hi, Munira! Have you seen the result?
Munira I've failed in my math test.
Shanu Oh, that's bad luck!
Munira —_ don’t know how to tell my parents.
Shanu T think they will understand.
I know you'll do better next time.
Language Focus
When we hear about any bad news, we say, 'I'm sorry to hear that’
or ‘That's bad luck’, etc. In response, the other person usually doesn't
say anything.
BI. Practise saying the above conversations in pairs.
B2. Answer the following questions.
What would you say to somebody who:
has won a lottery?
b has lost a football match?
c just congratulated you on your good result?
d__ has been ill?
e has been selected for a music programme on TV?
B3. Work with a partner. Make short conversations on the situations
given in B2.
cleanText:
6 English For Today
Let's now see what we say when we hear about something not good
or not happy. Read the following dialogues.
Bobby Hi, Sujan! Why didn’t you come
to school last week?
Sujan I had the flu. I was in bed
most of the time.
Bobby Oh, I'm sorry to hear that.
How do you feel now?
Shakil Hello, Manik! Why are you so quiet today?
Manik Did you see that pen my father gave me?
I've lost it. It was very dear to me!
Shakil Oh, I'm so sorry! How did you lose it?
Manik I don't know! I think I dropped it somewhere.
Shanu Hi, Munira! Have you seen the result?
Munira I've failed in my math test.
Shanu Oh, that's bad luck!
Munira —_ don’t know how to tell my parents.
Shanu T think they will understand.
I know you'll do better next time.
Language Focus
When we hear about any bad news, we say, 'I'm sorry to hear that’
or ‘That's bad luck’, etc. In response, the other person usually doesn't
say anything.
BI. Practise saying the above conversations in pairs.
B2. Answer the following questions.
What would you say to somebody who:
has won a lottery?
b has lost a football match?
c just congratulated you on your good result?
d__ has been ill?
e has been selected for a music programme on TV?
B3. Work with a partner. Make short conversations on the situations
given in B2.
